canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, the Multi-modality Benchmark (MMB) is consistently defined as a benchmark for evaluating Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs). It is characterized as providing a "robust and holistic evaluation" of these models. The primary behavior MMB is used to measure is Visual Question Answering (VQA), with one paper explicitly including it in a list of "conventional VQA tasks". In practice, MMB is frequently employed as part of a larger suite of evaluation tools. For instance, studies report using MMB alongside other widely-used MLLM benchmarks such as MME, MMMU, MathVista, and ScienceQA. This pattern of co-occurrence suggests its role as a common instrument within a broader set of evaluations for MLLMs.

EMMA: Empowering Multi-modal Mamba with Structural and Hierarchical Alignment
Routing Experts: Learning to Route Dynamic Experts in Existing Multi-modal Large Language Models
Visual Haystacks: A Vision-Centric Needle-In-A-Haystack Benchmark

definitions.text: Multi-modality Benchmark (MMB) is a benchmark that provides a robust and holistic evaluation of MLLMs.
definitions.paper: EMMA: Empowering Multi-modal Mamba with Structural and Hierarchical Alignment

relationships.paper: Visual Haystacks: A Vision-Centric Needle-In-A-Haystack Benchmark
relationships.evidence: ...we also evaluate MIRAGE on conventional VQA tasks, including the RetVQA multi-image QA (Penamakuri et al., 2023) and several single-image QA tasks, such as VQA-v2 (Goyal et al., 2017), GQA (Hudson & Manning, 2019), TextVQA (Singh et al., 2019), POPE (Li et al., 2023c), MMB (Liu et al., 2024c), MMB-CN (Liu et al., 2024c), MME (Fu et al., 2023), SEED-Bench (Li et al., 2023a), and MM-Vet (Yu et al., 2023b). (Section 5)
